parser_pitfalls: Mailbox owner is Ryan Slinger (ryan.slinger@enron.com), NOT Richard Slinger. The DATASET_CONTRACT example uses 'richard.slinger@enron.com' illustratively; do not treat that as the real owner address.
Automated/bulletin messages (e.g. Outage Reports) use placeholder senders like 'no.address@enron.com' and have an empty To: header.
Enron distribution-list expansion produces double-dot local parts such as 'm..driscoll@enron.com' and 'd..thomas@enron.com'; these are valid addresses in the corpus, not parse errors.
Clean inbox/sent_items/deleted_items split; good starter mailbox for header-field and recipient-role challenges.
Smallest mailbox (35 files); ideal for exact-lookup and Message-ID-discovery challenges with a tightly bounded search space.

A 'contacts' folder (2 files) exists alongside mail folders; its items are still RFC822 messages with Message-IDs, but they are not conversational email and should be excluded from participant/topic scopes.
No conventional 'inbox' folder: incoming mail lives in 'notes_inbox'; sent mail is split across 'sent' and '_sent_mail'. Do not assume inbox/sent_items naming when scoping challenges.

'all_documents' and 'discussion_threads' are Notes-style archive folders that heavily overlap/duplicate messages seen in other folders (same Message-ID content may appear in multiple folders); useful for mailbox-hygiene/duplicate-looking challenges but risky for naive counts.
Topic folders 'southern_cone' and 'southeast' are region-specific and good for bounded-topic material.

The 'inbox' contains NESTED subfolders (inbox/bankruptcy, inbox/rto_west, inbox/regulatory, inbox/ferc, inbox/rto, inbox/ubs). A recursive walk records source_folder as e.g. 'inbox/bankruptcy'; a challenge scoped to 'inbox' must state whether subfolders are in or out of bounds.
Largest Easy mailbox (519); regulatory/FERC/RTO subfolders provide clean topic-bounded material and many Re:/Fwd: threads.
'calendar' and 'to_do' folders hold non-conversational Notes items with Message-IDs; exclude from participant/topic scopes.

The 'pictures' folder holds emails that MENTION image attachments (e.g. body lists ' - MVC-003S.JPG') but the actual JPEG bytes are NOT present. Perfect for attachment-mention challenges that distinguish a text mention from a real MIME attachment.
No 'inbox' folder: mail is split between 'all_documents' (archive, 199) and 'sent' (189). 'all_documents' duplicates much of the mailbox, so avoid using it for exact counts.
Personal/off-Enron correspondents appear (e.g. ssmgranmayeh@msn.com, obgynassociates.com), so From/To are not always @enron.com addresses.
